extra.message: User input is passed directly to os.system() in do_os_system(), which executes arbitrary OS commands. This function is called from multiple web framework route handlers with unsanitized user input.
User input is passed directly to subprocess.Popen() with shell=True, allowing arbitrary OS command execution. This function is called from web route handlers with unsanitized user input.

User input is formatted directly into SQL string using Python string format() in do_sqlite3_execute(). The query 'INSERT INTO Character VALUES ('{}', '1')' is vulnerable to SQL injection via the user_input parameter.

User input is formatted directly into SQL string using Python string format() in do_sqlite3_executemany(). The query 'INSERT INTO Character VALUES ('{}', ?)' is vulnerable to SQL injection via the user_input parameter.

User input is formatted directly into SQL string using Python string format() in do_sqlite3_executescript(). The query 'INSERT INTO Character VALUES ('{}', '1'); SELECT 0' is vulnerable to SQL injection. executescript() is particularly dangerous as it supports multiple SQL statements.
User input is passed to pickle.load() in do_pickle_load(). Deserializing untrusted data with pickle allows arbitrary code execution via crafted pickle payloads.
User input is passed to pickle.loads() in do_pickle_loads(). Deserializing untrusted data with pickle allows arbitrary code execution via crafted pickle payloads.
User input is passed to yaml.load() with yaml.UnsafeLoader in do_yaml_load(). Using UnsafeLoader allows arbitrary Python object instantiation and code execution.
User input is passed to yaml.load_all() with yaml.UnsafeLoader in do_yaml_load_all(). Using UnsafeLoader allows arbitrary Python object instantiation and code execution.
User input is passed directly to exec() in do_exec(). This allows arbitrary Python code execution.
User input is passed directly to eval() in do_eval(). This allows arbitrary Python expression evaluation and code execution.
User input is passed directly to compile() in do_compile(). The compiled code object can be executed to run arbitrary Python code.

User input is passed to lxml.etree.fromstring() in do_lxml_etree_fromstring(). lxml's default parser resolves external entities, enabling XML External Entity (XXE) attacks that can read local files or perform SSRF.
User input is passed to xml.dom.pulldom.parseString() in do_xml_dom_pulldom_parsestring(). The default XML parser in Python's standard library is vulnerable to XXE attacks.
User input is passed to xml.sax.parseString() in do_xml_sax_parsestring(). The default SAX parser does not disable external entity processing, enabling XXE attacks.
User input is passed as URL to urlopen() in do_legacy_urlopen(). An attacker can control the URL to make the server send requests to arbitrary internal or external hosts (SSRF).
User input is passed as URL string to urlopen() in do_urlopen_str(). An attacker can control the URL to make the server send requests to arbitrary internal or external hosts.
User input is passed to Request() constructor and then to urlopen() in do_urlopen_obj(). An attacker can control the URL to make the server send requests to arbitrary hosts.
User input is passed as URL path to HTTPConnection.request() in do_httpconnection_request_url(). An attacker can control the request URL.
User input is passed as HTTP method to HTTPConnection.request() in do_httpconnection_request_method(). An attacker can control the HTTP method used in the request.
User input is passed as URL to HTTPConnection.putrequest() in do_httpconnection_putrequest_url(). An attacker can control the request URL.
User input is passed as HTTP method to HTTPConnection.putrequest() in do_httpconnection_putrequest_method(). An attacker can control the HTTP method.
User input is passed as URL to HTTPSConnection.request() in do_httpsconnection_request_url(). An attacker can control the request URL.
User input is passed as HTTP method to HTTPSConnection.request() in do_httpsconnection_request_method(). An attacker can control the HTTP method.
User input is passed as URL to HTTPSConnection.putrequest() in do_httpsconnection_putrequest_url(). An attacker can control the request URL.
User input is passed as HTTP method to HTTPSConnection.putrequest() in do_httpsconnection_putrequest_method(). An attacker can control the HTTP method.
User input is passed as host to HTTPConnection() constructor in do_httpconnection_init(). An attacker can control which host the server connects to.
User input is passed as host to HTTPSConnection() constructor in do_httpsconnection_init(). An attacker can control which host the server connects to.
User input is passed as file path to tarfile.TarFile.open() in do_tarfile_open(). An attacker can read arbitrary files via path traversal (e.g., '../../etc/passwd').
User input is passed as file path to tarfile.TarFile() constructor in do_tarfile_class(). An attacker can read arbitrary tar files via path traversal.
User input is passed as file path to tarfile.open() with bz2 mode in do_tarfile_bz2(). An attacker can read arbitrary bz2-compressed tar files via path traversal.
User input is passed as file path to bz2.BZ2File() in do_bz2_class(). An attacker can read arbitrary bz2 files via path traversal.
User input is passed as file path to bz2.open() in do_bz2_open(). An attacker can read arbitrary bz2 files via path traversal.
User input is passed as file path to io.open() in do_io_open(). An attacker can read arbitrary files on the filesystem via path traversal.
User input is passed as file path to open() in do_open(). An attacker can read arbitrary files on the filesystem via path traversal.
User input is passed directly to lxml root.findall() in do_lxml_etree_findall(). An attacker can inject XPath expressions to extract data from the XML document.
User input is passed directly to lxml root.findtext() in do_lxml_etree_findtext(). An attacker can inject XPath expressions.
User input is passed directly to xml.etree.ElementTree node.findall() in do_xml_etree_elementtree_findall(). An attacker can inject XPath expressions.

User input is rendered with the Jinja2 '| safe' filter which disables auto-escaping: {{ user_input | safe }}. This allows arbitrary HTML/JavaScript injection leading to cross-site scripting.
User input from query parameter is concatenated into command string and passed to os.system() in the /cmdi endpoint: cmd = 'echo ' + user_input and os.system(cmd). This enables OS command injection.

Uploaded file content (first 20 bytes) is passed directly to do_os_system() (which calls os.system()) in the /files/upload endpoint. An attacker can upload a file with shell commands to achieve OS command injection.

Uploaded file content (first 10 bytes) is concatenated into command string and passed to os.system() in the FileUpload handler: cmd = 'echo ' + str(user_input[:10]) and os.system(cmd). This enables OS command injection.

User input from query parameter is concatenated into command string and passed to os.system() in a background thread: cmd = 'echo ' + str(user_input) and os.system(cmd). This enables OS command injection via the /thread endpoint.

Uploaded file content (first 20 bytes) is passed to do_os_system() (which calls os.system()) in the /files/upload endpoint. An attacker can upload a file with shell commands to achieve OS command injection.

The regex pattern r'((a)+)+' is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS). This pattern has nested quantifiers that cause catastrophic backtracking when matching certain input strings. User input is matched against this pattern in multiple functions (do_re_match, do_re_search, do_re_findall, etc.).

cwe: CWE-400: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

extra.message: Django DEBUG mode is set to True. In production, this exposes detailed error pages with stack traces, local variables, and settings to attackers.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as 'test_key'. A known secret key compromises session security, CSRF protection, and any cryptographic signing in Django.
Flask SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE is explicitly set to False. Session cookies will be sent over unencrypted HTTP connections, allowing session hijacking via network sniffing.

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

extra.message: Flask SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY is explicitly set to False. Session cookies can be accessed by client-side JavaScript, making them vulnerable to theft via XSS.
Cache-Control header is set to 'public' without 'no-cache' or 'no-store' directives. Sensitive responses may be cached by intermediate proxies or browsers, exposing sensitive data.

cwe: CWE-525: Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0', disabling the browser's built-in XSS filter. While this header is deprecated in modern browsers, disabling it removes an additional layer of XSS defense.
Strict-Transport-Security header max-age is set to 0, which effectively disables HSTS. This allows downgrade attacks from HTTPS to HTTP.
MD5 hash function is used in do_hashlib_md5(). MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for security-sensitive operations like password hashing or integrity verification.
SHA1 hash function is used in do_hashlib_sha1(). SHA1 is considered cryptographically weak with known collision attacks and should not be used for security-sensitive operations.
hashlib.new('SHA1') creates a SHA1 hash in do_hashlib_new(). SHA1 is considered cryptographically weak with known collision attacks.
MD5 is used to compute a digest of uploaded file content: md5(user_input).digest(). MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for integrity verification.

Python's random module is used for generating random numbers in do_random(). The random module uses a Mersenne Twister PRNG which is not cryptographically secure and should not be used for security-sensitive purposes like token generation.

cwe: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

extra.message: Jinja2 Environment is created without enabling autoescape (autoescape defaults to False). Templates rendered with this environment will not auto-escape HTML entities, enabling XSS when user input is rendered in templates.
